reviews.review_text:
This game has flaws. It's levels are procedurally generated. We're playing coop and getting "dead end fatigue" for when we clear yet another illogical maze of a map and can't find our way back to the souls-like grace/bonfire equivalent that would allow us to fast travel. We got lost to a point that we had to use the "liquid escape" item - essentially kill ourselves to get back to the checkpoint. Sure, there is a map, except it doesn't show which door the random level generation decided to leave open this time... Also, the game has statues that heal you - and the dumb procedural generation likes to place them near each other, instead of spreading them out throughout the dungeon. Because of these reasons - I'd give it a mixed review, if steam had that option. 

Besides that - gunplay feels smooth, music is beautiful (when it decides to actually play, which isn't often for some reason), and the biome design and variety is stunning. It's unusual (in a good kind of way) to play a game that throws you in a fantasy setting in one level, some grim Victorian plague village in the next, and then something sci-fi-esque.

Such an underrated game. Absolutely a blast to play. While the main story is very mehh, the "side stories" in the game are incredible writing and world building. There's always new stuff to find, and I feel like every time I do a new run I see something I've never seen before.

At launch I got some stutters, but it runs amazingly these days.

Awesome game and probably one of the best Souls-Like out there. The Co-Op especially is super fun with friends. Lots of Loot to find, awesome weapons like the Monorail or the Genesis bow and the ability to Multi-Class gives it a lot of character/build depth. Game is quite challenging on the higher difficulties and the amount of hidden items/secrets and alternate rewards is mind-boggling.

I really hope we will get a sequel at some point again

Do you like a good Soulslike? Do you like shooting guns in video games? Do you like fun takes on scifi and horror tropes? Then you're gonna like this game. A lot. It's more polished than it's predecessor in a lot of important ways. It is also extremely easy to play with your pals, which makes it even better. Your player characters will converse with each other, even! Wild!

Just because a game has dodge mechanic and hard hitting mobs/bosses, doesn't mean it is a souls-like. The game felt boring with unnecessarily huge maps and very limited weapon choices. And then there's the procedural generation of maps and weapons locked behind them. So if u're looking for a specific weapon and u didn't get the map this run then waiting until new game is your only option. The game is designed to be replayed with the very limited content it has to offer and if u can manage to not get bored during the very first run then maybe u'll find it fun later on. I however couldn't make it through the first run itself
Fun game can recommend, needs a wiki open on your second monitor tho if you wanna collect a bunch of stuff scattered throughout regular runs even.
ดีกว่าที่คิดไว่้มากปกติ ชอบเล่นแนวโซลเปลี่ยนมาใช่ปืน สนุกไปอีกแบบ
I like the part where you shoot fricking lazer beams at God x100 just to get his finger so you can shoot fricking lazer beams at God 2 faster
Love the game and all of its upgrades for the original.
Its very fun but short. I wish there had been 5 or more planets. The dungeons are pretty good. Trying to collect all the items is interesting and the puzzles in the dungeons are fun.

A good co-op shooter with lots of freedom to craft your own playstyle. The fun in this game is the gameplay loop, exploration and the puzzles although some can be quite cryptic to figure out.

Few downside are that I won't be able to find everything on my own and I have to do a lot of wiki reading, and there's a lot of bugs present in this game like crashing because of memory leak, skills not triggering properly, etc.

This is honestly a very fun game with tons of replayability. I've already replayed it a couple of times and I'm still finding new items, secrets, and achievements. The variety of builds, weapons, and worlds keeps every playthrough feeling fresh. Whether you're playing solo or with friends, there's always something new to discover. Highly recommend it to anyone who enjoys action RPGs and challenging combat. Also runs great on a gaming laptop. :)

I was a fan of and played this on console before switching to PC, so I've 100% this game twice now. This game has a noticeable Souls-like style with "bonfires" and enemy respawn mechanics, plus the emphasis on boss fights. The reward you get for game knowledge in this game is significantly faster scrap grind as if you pick up an item you already gotten that is your reward. The amount and depth of secrets and hidden items in this game is baffling.

 - Find a handful of vaguely edible items? give them to the chef and get unique consumables and eventually a hidden amulet.
 - Run a very specific build and you can find yourself in the backrooms to get a "hidden" class.
 - Kill a specific boss with a specific weapon and you get their crown.

Movement and controls are pretty responsive too and leave a ton of space for skill expression. Can take a bit to get used to though.

Build variety is one of the best out there, and with the variance of difficulty settings (plus rewards for challenging yourself) you can experiment with builds and eventually optimize for more challenging modes. There are even rewards for completing worlds and entire runs up to veteran difficulty in hardcore (permadeath).
 
I greatly recommend this game to secret hunters and build-crafters!

Really enjoyed the game, the fps souls like is refreshing. The randomness and wide variety of builds, weapons and combining archetypes is just amazing making every run different. The most amazing about this game is how well the developers hid the 'rewards' in every run. Just getting the final archetype archon is already so satisfying.
Now on apoc diff because i just cant stop. Everything in the game is so rewarding, maybe I'll stop once i get all the hidden rewards.

i would go with neutral if i could. its a fine game but it clearly feels like it was made for Co-Op. i played it solo and coop and solo feels so much worse in some moments if you get swarmed by enemys etc. get it when its on sale or play with a homie.

I never played multiplayer in this game and im still having such a blast playing the game solo, It has such an adventurous feel, the perfect mix of wonder/puzzles/combat. I just can't stop playing the game because it just keeps me on edge of wanting to see more of new stuff, even though it's randomly generated. Even though this is a souls-like, It has it's own work of magic in it that will make you feel like it isn't just an another bland souls-like.

Relatively complex itemization especially compared to the first game. It borderline has too many secrets. If you like co-op games with secrets it's a no brainer. If you only want straight forward gameplay without any RNG maybe not for you.
Amazing game, Boss fights are extremely fun and fairly difficult. so far seems to hold up to the fun from the first but has some nice new features that help you personalize your character.

i dont even know where to begin. This game has given me memories that i will never forget. Has fun replay ability. Many classes to choose from and fun builds to try and test as well as challenging the player. I cannot recommend this game enough. the worst part about this game though is that it had to end. 10/10

I loved playing the first game and am enjoying this one even more. It is a great game solo and with friends. 
 

BUY IT!!!!!!

It's a fun game but be careful depending on what build you do may ♥♥♥♥ you because they wanna lock ♥♥♥♥ behind Hardcore ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ since for some reason they decided to have a stick up their ass and think that some people actually want to play hardcore :/

Really enjoyed the first games, but the second one doesn't give me the same thriller to overcrowded enemies. Fighting the boss feel easy if you play with friends even at the high difficulty as the boss doesn't spawn any enemies to shielded them anymore. First remnant multiplies the number of enemies spawned based on total player in the games to help the boss. 

However, this game still considered epic to me
Very fun game. Highly recommend playing without looking anything up for the first campaign play through. Exploration is top tier.
Love this game, best with buddies, but still fun solo. Don't be afraid to turn up the difficulty.

i loved this game so much. there is so much replayablity that even when trying to get that one world to get an achievment it doesnt feel like a grind it just feels rewarding. the main issue i have rn is that no matter what i try to do my game just wont load i click play and then it loads a black screen and then closes out. but overall i recommend this game to everyone
Gun go bang, and I fart. 9/10, its great. snag it on sale.

Good Game. If you have plan on doing multiplayer playthrough, don't do normal difficulty. It will genuinely be the easiest playthrough. There are few moments of difficult still. But what is supposed to be a 25+ hour game turned into a 15 hour experience with friends because the game seems to not really compensate for the increase number of players, at least on normal difficulty. The gameplay and bosses are still an amazing experience. But if you play with friends you have to play on the harder difficulties unless you want that easy experience.

We need Remnant 3.

The gameplay and story is too good to let settle for soo long and many modern games could learn a lot from the great gameplay here.

o7

Lots to do, lots of content, this game is a lot of fun and especially with friends. Its a difficult game but nothing beats trying the boss again for the 20th time. The game could be better optimized its the only game that really drags my PC and this can run most games I play really well except the very poorly optimized ones.

Remnant 2 Review
A Fun but Frustrating Experience
Remnant 2 is a game that checks many of the right boxes. Its stunning visuals and immersive atmosphere make it a unique experience, and its mechanics are decent, if not particularly innovative. However, the game's design choices, particularly when it comes to navigation, can be infuriatingly frustrating.

The Good:
• Immersive Atmosphere: The game's atmosphere, with a haunting soundtrack.
• Decent Mechanics: The game's combat and character customization systems are solid, if not particularly groundbreaking and challenging boss battles are fun.

The Bad:
• Re-Treading Your Path: the game's lack of clear navigation and poor mapping system can make it feel like you're constantly re-tracing your steps. This can be incredibly boring and frustrating, especially when you're trying to explore the game's world.

The Verdict:
Remnant 2 is a game that's fun to play, but its frustrating navigation and poor mapping system hold it back from being a truly great experience. If you're a fan of action RPGs and don't mind getting lost in a beautiful but poorly designed world, then Remnant 2 might be for you. However, if you're looking for a game with a more intuitive navigation system, you might want to look elsewhere.
Rating: 7/10

It’s actually really fun. The story is a bit lame, but this game is really more about exploration and solving puzzles anyway. It’s a bit of a shame that the devs didn't integrate the puzzles and all the exploration more seamlessly to extend the playtime. You can only find some things, like new Archetypes, by Googling them or actively farming for them and that’s a real pity. because some of those elements, it would have felt so much cooler if they had been implemented differently. Sure, you can still go out and do it, but a lot of people playing this game will likely just run through the campaign and then put it down.

Grab this if you like the souls genre and guns.

This is just as fun as the first game, which is to say a lot. I really enjoy third-person shooters, especially mixed with some RPG or roguelite. It took a minute or two for the new archtype system to grow on me, but I've come to really appreciate it. 

Lots of reasons to replay the game. Even after getting most everything it is always fun to jump in with friends or even randos! The soudntrack by Rob Westwood is amazing. Great motifs you'll recognize throughout the game starting from the main menu.

If you have an Intel GPU as I did earlier (b580), Remnant 2 does have Intel XeSS support.
Best souls-like game with guns. Gameplay loop is great with a lot of replayability.

[h3]เกมแนว Soul-like ที่ให้ความรู้สึกคล้ายเกมแนว ARPG[/h3]

รีวิวหลังเล่นจบรอบแรก ระดับยากธรรมดา
- เกมดีระดับหนึ่งเลย เปิดมาให้เลือกคลาสที่แตกต่างกัน มีสกิลเซ็ตที่ไม่เหมือนกัน สามารถใช้คลาสได้ 2 คลาสพร้อมกันได้ สร้างบิลด์ที่อยากเล่นได้ เป็นจุดที่ชอบที่สุดของเกม ของสวมใส่เสริมพลังเยอะและเกมขยันแจก 
- แผนที่ใช้ random generate ถ้าแยกกันเล่นกับเพื่อนก็อาจได้ของแตกต่างกันไปเลยตั้งแต่เริ่มเกม สามารถสุ่มแผนที่เพื่อหาของที่ขาดไปได้ เกมจึงออกแนวกึ่งๆ rogue-like
- ช่วงแรกเกมค่อนข้างยากตายซ้ำตายซากตลอด แต่หลังจากเลเวลสูงได้ของเยอะบิลด์ครบก็ง่ายไปเลย ช่วงหลังเข้าเกมไปยิงศัตรูเล่นๆ เอามันส์ยังได้
- เนื้อเรื่อง? คืออะไรหรอ ไม่ได้โฟกัส

ปล. ใครเล่นแล้วเมากล้อง ให้ไปปรับ option ไม่ใส่จอสั่นก่อน จะเล่นอย่างมีความสุขมากขึ้น

Absolutely fantastic game, if you like Dark Souls and like guns you'll love this as it's basically Dark Souls with guns. 

Maps are randomly generated from tile sets so no two playthrough are identical as the layout and which bosses and dungeons spawn change every time and that variety grows even further if you grab the 3 DLC's. 

I will say however, some of the 'hidden' weapons are incredibly convoluted to discover and it's very unlikely you'll find a lot of stuff without looking up a guide. (EG one item you need to dig in three very exact spots in 3 separate locations that need to spawn for your current run and the game basically leaves you to guess where those spots are)

But other than that, there's still plenty of guns and loot you'll find wandering about. The game has a total of 14 classes to choose from and after a bit of story progression you can mix two of those classes together to create unique builds to fit any kind of playstyle which is then boosted further by weapons, weapon mods that have special effects alongside mutators to give stat bonuses (EG a sniper with a mutator that boosts damage the longer you aim or a shotgun that does a burst of AOE damage when you equip it) but there's like 70+ main guns and probably the same amount of secondary guns so functionally endless combinations of class skills, weapon mods, weapon mutators, rings and necklaces that change how you play.

Cons: Performance can be a bit hit and miss and the RNG on what dungeons you get (And therefore what items you get in your run) might drive some people crazy but the game is designed to be replayed over and over and there's an 'adventure' mode where you can roll a specific world separate from your campaign progression to make it far easier to hunt down a specific item

Absolutely fantastic game and by far my favourite Souls-like game
very fun game and playing it completely with friends. Love seeing my friends fall of the map each time the jump leads into a roll

A shooting souls-like game with intentional reruns of the game to get dungeon rolls and drop rolls. I played this game co-op and the game is platinum'd and 99% of the items were acquired. Most of the runs were done on Apoc and DLCs were played and completed.  


Pros: 
- not particularly difficult, even on Apocalypse. The core gameplay loop is to roll a run with pre-determined maps and dungeons and to finish the run, just to reroll again numerous times to get everything in a world. This got stale real fast considering how many times it had to be done. 
- boss design was good for the most part though dungeon bosses are reused from regular elite enemies. Not many bosses are ads-focused so that was pleasant and on average, it took less than an hour to kill most bosses on Apoc. 
- lots of diversity in armaments and equipment, perhaps a little too much diversity? But it allowed for some interesting build combos like a status-focused one, explosion, shield, summons, etc. 
- archetypes were good. They were different enough from each other that they had their own playstyle and build around it. 
- You don't need good aim. It's slightly unfortunate that many of the weapons just straight up suck. The mods too; only a handful were actually good like hot shot or stasis beam. Lots of fixed mods to the weapon too. The mutators are also ok; cool gimmick. Lots of the melee weapons. Krell axe probs the best with a status build and this just shows how insane meta weapons were over other weapons despite how much weapon diversity there was. 
- fragments and prisms were a nice touch to the game. It came naturally and it added a good bonus (if mythic). The RNG aspect of it though was ehhhhhhhhhhh. The legendary upgrades of the prism was good if you get a good stat. If you get an awful stat (most of the buffs), you just have to reroll and the RNG of that was sad; RNG of regular fragments was fine. 
- lots of diversity in relics but tbh, most of them are pointless. Some of them have a niche and that niche can be good if you build around it but, it still pales over the standard dragon heart or salvaged heart. Having the options is nice but it doesn't matter if all your options are dookie. At least they tried. 


Cons: 
- poorly optimized with frequent memory leaks and crashing. Frame drop is ridiculous and cannot consistently get 75 frames without going to potato graphics quality and frame generation. Frame capping does not work in-game; you set a frame limit and it don't matter. 
- the whole gimmick is replayability which is fine by itself, but some events require other events to occur in the same run. Yes you have adventure mode and that is absolutely necessary, but some events (like the nightweaver web or lurking dran event) require a ridiculous amount of RNG and rerolling. It's not a game breaker bc these are all optionals but the rewards for many of them aren't even that good so it's not even rewarding. 
- SO STUPID THAT THE MAP IS NOT SHARED AMONG COOP PLAYERS (EVEN THOUGH IT WAS IN FTA), AND THERE'S NO COMPASS. The ping system just doesn't work. Even considering the 3D environment of the game, the ping just doesn't implement well. It's crazy how items are shared upon acquiring it and players can join another server and share that progress, but MAP EXPLORATION IS NOT SHARED. 
- You gotta play with the wiki up if you want to get the stuff. A ton of missables and it's a real shame that if you want to play the game and get stuff, you need a second monitor with the wiki pulled up that tells you what you missed. It's not fun to have to interrupt your gaming session to read an article on stuff that you missed and then subsequently will have to reroll, or spoil yourself on the blind run. 
- music, sfx, and voice acting is just meh. Bc of the nature of doing multiple reruns, everything gets stale. 
- fashion was kinda forgettable. It would've been nice if you could actually fashion yourself without such a restricting encumbrance. Also, there is not armour set bonus like in FTA. 
- for how many traits there are in the game, you can only get a max of 85 additional trait upgrades. So that's a real shame considering how many traits there are in the game. 
- storyline is unimpressive but the world design was cool for the most part. Yaesha was reused from FTA but had a cool blood moon gimmick, Nerud was too open and empty, labyrinth was meh, losomn was cool, root earth was good ig. 


DLC 1 - The Awakened King 8/10
- shorter in content so it's less grindy in that sense, but it was cool. Boss was annoying af but the overall story was more interesting than other DLCs. 
- gives access to ritualist archetype which is so good for a status build. The whole DLC introduced a lot of status items. 
- not many bosses added but a fair amount of armaments that are at least decent. 

DLC 2 - The Forgotten Kingdom 6/10
- Event drops and reroll gimmick is especially unfortunate in this DLC. Lydusa itself is not a particularly interesting fight but there is a fair amount of added bosses. 
- invoker archetype was meh. Lowkey forgettable. 
- nothing memorable about any of the armaments but tbf, there's SOOOO MANY RINGS AND AMULETS AND WEAPONS. 

DLC 3 - The Dark Horizon 6/10
- uses a lot of kite mechanics to fly around. Really emphasizes how open the map was. Environmentally, it's a step up from base Nerud. 
- gives access to warden archetype which is good. The DLC added a lot of shield-based items which has an interesting build around it. Overall, not many added bosses but a ton of added items that spans other worlds. 
- added spark which was an interesting gimmick that incentivized people to rerun maps and dungeons. A little scavenger hunt of sorts. 


Overall, a good game for uber casuals and lowkey, good PC setups. It's not particularly difficult but offers a nice enough challenge. DLCs are required for platinum but by itself are ok for the most part. The game is good but not without its lapses. Obviously get it on sale bc ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ base price is hefty but hey, there's a lot of hours of content that is optional even with platinum achievements.

Since I make a lot of negative reviews, I decided to make a positive for once.

It's Dark Souls with Guns. It's way better then Remnant I, which often felt clanky. Is it without faults? No. But it is ejoyable. Grab a friend (the game feels way better in co-op) and start blasting!
Story is crap, the game insist that you should care about some Girl With Magical Powers and Girl Who Saved Your Life Outside of the Screen View or an Old Fart that Simply Peaces Out Cause He's Bored! But there is no emotion there.

On a positive note, do not worry about the story. No really. Don't.
Because the ammount of classes, weapons, upgrades, trinkets and builds is A LOT! Frankly it takes a while to make your build the way you want, but when it clicks, it's gorgeous.
You wanna blask with guns? Sure.
You wanna be a mage? Which flavour? We have lot's of those.
You wanna be unkillable tank? Easy enough.
You wanna cosplay as Palpatine? Yup we have it.

There's a lot of locations to explore and forget what I said about story... Cause there's a bunch of interesting stories (just not the main one, that one is still trash)! The first time you explore those new worlds it's gonna be a memorable experience.
And after 160+ hours I keep comming back to kill some more bosses and make more builds. :)

The gameplay itself was solid at a mechanical level. 
The keyboard keybinds do take a moment to adjust to, but once adjusted for it does make sense and start to feel comfortable playing. 
The story felt fractured especially after the first game where the story was the forefront of gameplay. 
Despite the fracture story, the exploration feels fresh and enjoyable. 
Major fights feel spaced out well, with tankier enemies taking the place of the more common boss fights found in Remnant from the Ashes. 
The things just hidden around are so much fun to either stumble upon or deep dive to find and discover. 
A good an enjoyable game that keeps your attention and challenges your skill level.
What an awesome game. Even better than Remnant from the Ashes and that game absolutely rocked!

If youre allergic to spice stay away cause I have got a hot take:
I prefer the first Remnant but I do recommend this game, quick comparrison
World- Remnant 2 has a large variaty but I dont like most of it, the labyrinth isnt great, Nerud isnt great and so on Remnant 1 takes it
Gameplay-2 is way more intricit, complex and more rewarding 2 takes this one.
Playthrough- 1 is in my opnion better in terms of pacing, flow of the game, gear aquisition is more natural and overall 1 kept me wanting to play more while each world of 2 was a gamble either I liked it or I couldnt wait to be done with it.
Bosse-2 takes this one although some boss mechanics were crazy iykyk
Gear- while 2 offers more gear overall and more options to choose from I think some times more isnt really better, I alway looked around and only found rings, amulets and relics. VERY VERY rarely I found weapons and armor, most of them you buy or craft at ward 13 which I did not like. More over material for upgrades were far to little in my opnion I played close to 30 hours and only got my main weapon to +15 and in remnant 1 in 19 hours all my gear was maxed out to 1 takes this point.
Over all this is a fine game if I gave remnant 1 and 7-8 Id have to give this a 6-7.
Its borderlands, destiny, and dark souls put together and it works. Multiplayer is pretty simple to use. The bosses are big, unique, and difficult. bought on sale but worth the premium.
requires epic games to be installed on your computer to play cross play even if neither of you bought the game on epic games

spent 30 mins stuck in the first town cuz it refused to flag the quest from hank.. finally after mutlabie reload, reinstalls. it flagged!!!! finally i can play the game right... right? nope. i donno what that beeping thing is but i never want to hear it again. killed the stuff that came when i clicked e on it. and it just beeped and beeped and beeped  and beeped. oh you are on a quest? whats a quest log? did you not hear what they said while they were telling you to do something improtent. good luck figureing that out
Spammy bosses, micromanaging mechanics as an excuse for exciting gameplay
A slow burn story with easy PvE mechanics that you can gradually build on. No playthrough is the same twice, and even the sweatiest of sweats will find a reason to come back.

With 10.5 hours played, my biggest question is how did I miss the gem?  Incredible, souls like in that you use a check point system and the tension of having to go on with little health and ammo is spot on.  If you are like me and prefer a mouse and keyboard, this game really is smooth.  It handles gamepad nicely if you are into that but know this is entirely playable the "old fashioned way".   The replay ability is immense.  Worth it!

TL;DR: BLEEEEEEEAAAAARGH.

I tried to like it. But I ended up hating it more and more with every hour played. Not sure if hate is the right word though - too strong. I think what this game made me feel about it was worse - apathy.

I picked a Handler, as - well, it seemed like a rather obvious choice for solo-play. And the dog felt if not entirely useless - but more like hit and miss. With 8/10 - miss. When most enemies are flying aliens above ground, and you have a DOG for a combat buddy..... Talk about bringing your knife to a gun-fight.

Speaking of guns. I found myself bored to tears by gunplay, which seemed adequate at the start - but eventually felt like a chore. There's no good feedback as you're shooting enemies - you just mow them down, as they dissolve into paste. There are no chunky, snappy death animations, or hit-reactions, no real rhythm to combat - enemies come on to you like nausea, a mass that you shoot at, until it subsides. There is no feeling of satisfaction to match your triumphs. The loot doesn't do it, but more on that later.

Perhaps it's due to the fact that it was build with co-op in mind, and having a buddy or two - watching each others' back. But there's something off that felt about the combat, which irritated me to no end. Yeah, you do have a melee attack. And your two guns. But the enemies in melee tend to swarm up on you and I don't have an adequate tool to deal with them, other than melee - which is a total crapshoot in terms of accuracy, or trying to "aim" it.

That "multiplayer" vibe, if you will, is what eventually drained me of any will to continue this slog. It constantly feels like you're playing with an unknown handicap, and there's ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ you can do about it.

Inventory system. I kept picking stuff up, looking at vendor gear. But after taking a look at those "stats", I couldn't be bothered upgrading or swapping any of my gear around - as the item comparison system is utterly dreadful. It sort of shows the stats you gain and ones you lose - but that's literally it. You don't see the value deltas - the amount lost or gained. So you're forced to open your inventory and memorize what you've got, before shopping. I mean - even bloody Borderlands had a better inventory system (and I don't mean that as a compliment by the way).

Story. Well they got the voice-over right, gotta give them something. Listening to some of the NPCs in town was a real treat. That said, a few of them just unload 10-15 minute monologues on you. The game loves its lore-dumps and you learn that good an early. But in the few hours of my time spent in Remnant 2 - I never felt hooked on the story, never felt that feel of chasing the "what's next" It felt so diluted and lacking direction that I eventually stopped caring about that too.

So what can I say after 9 hours? It's not great, probably not even terrible. But that is its biggest sin in my opinion - nothing in it is Fun.

If you hate Dark Souls because the camera is just not janky enough, Bed of Chaos is not annoying enough, and think it would be better if it had guns, Oblivion style level scaling, and more visual clutter, then this game is for you.
Runs and looks OK on my PC.
Story suck azz and so short, game play fun, but yeah the story was more than enough to make me put a negative review.

The intro is a black woman crying about White male privilege in a post apocalyptic world where 99.9% of humanity is dead and the remaining 0.1% are under constant siege in a perpetually violent hellscape. You cannot skip this at all, you are forced to listen to the some mentally ill developer's self insert for at least 5 minutes, it was one of the dumbest things a game has ever tried to force me to sit through since crystal dynamics tomb raider reboot's token fat retarded asian companion trying to tell lara croft that the tomb raider game series isn't about her.

Also the gameplay is infinitely worse than it was in the 1st game. Have a nice day.

While I'm not even mad because I got the bundle on sale and the very good part one of the game for free, part two is a textbook example for what happens to a game when you let the woke mindvirus infest the devteam.

DEI-characters complaining about old white men, boring "story", forgettable npcs and enemies, unbalanced weapons, mods, boss mobs jumping you in the middle of maps - just urgh
Watch as most of the items you need to progress or to have disappear to a known bug forcing you to reroll and start all over.

I love this game, but the fact that I can't run this game at a stable framerate on medium settings is unacceptable.

The combat is fun, but honestly, the maps are just so horrible in this game that I'm going to not recommend it. I thought the maps were bland in the first one... the desert world in 2 is just one of the worst zones I've ever seen in a video game. Two maps that are large, barren wastelands full of nothing but enemies that spawn just so you have something to do. There's no direction as to where to go. There's a marker for the main quest, which kind of just points in every direction. If you want the engineer class you actually have to explore the entire desert looking for a specific stalagmite pattern in a randomly generated location and walk THROUGH the poisonous fog (that slows you down and makes your character puke so who would think of going that far in the first place??) to find it. I'm going to finish the game and my partner and I will probably have fun with it but honestly, that god awful desert world and the boring randomly generated maps are so bad that I legitimately think it's worth not buying the game, even on sale.
Beautiful game, lets make remnant 3 open world?

Although there were no mayor dealbreakers for me, the accumulation of bugs, weird design choices of enemies/worlds/mechanics, and the rather horrible feeling movement (with the dodge-rolls as a highlight), I cannot straight-up recommend this game.

note: I think this experience may be the result of me expecting a "Dark Souls with guns". A lot of mechanics that are also found in Dark Souls are executed rather differently, and notably a lot worse, in my opinion. 

Overall I feel like the game has a very promising premise, but a horrible execution. If you do plan on getting this game, I would recommend looking into the more niche mechanics and design choices of this game (how dodge rolling works, how you cannot level armor, how world/power level scaling works).

is it better then the first one yes

is it a good shooter yes
am i good no

I love shooters, souls likes, and rogue likes, Remnant 2 does a good job of meshing the three together. Lots of build variety. Lots of replayability. Unique boss fights. Nothing feels impossible or overly frustrating. The worlds are each beautiful in their own ways. Almost too many secrets and hidden areas, you always feel like you may have missed something and want to check every inch of every map. 

Decent story but sub-par writing. Heavy on the exposition. A bit more mystery wouldn't hurt. Many NPCs have robotic, forgettable dialogue (due to the writing, not voice acting). The game feels the need to walk you through the history of every area instead of using context clues or your imagination.

Performance is solid. Only tanks for me in Malefic Gallery, strangely enough. Pretty small place and not heavy on VFX so I'm not sure what the deal is there. The rest of it ran smoothly. 

Just came back to it for the third time and likely not the last. 

Too many flying enemies.

7.5/10
